title: Presidential Advisor: “I Directly Handled UFO Material” (Ft. Harald Malmgren)
summary: Harold Malmgren, a presidential adviser to JFK, LBJ, Nixon, and Ford, discusses his involvement in the Cuban Missile Crisis, his handling of UFO material that fell from a nuclear test in the Marshall Islands, and briefings he received on other-world technologies from CIA officials. He also discusses connections between UFOs, nuclear weapons programs, and the JFK assassination.

acronyms.definition: Modern term for UFOs used by government and military
US Department of Defense agency responsible for development of emerging technologies
Department of Energy security clearance for access to nuclear weapons information, managed by the Atomic Energy Commission
Long-range nuclear missile capable of reaching targets across continents
Second highest military alert level, one step below nuclear war

events.description: Cuban Missile Crisis - Harold Malmgren served as liaison between McNamara, Bundy, and JFK in the situation room, helping to de-escalate tensions with Curtis LeMay
Operation Dominic nuclear tests in Marshall Islands, including Bluegill Triple Prime test where UFO material was reportedly recovered
Strategic Air Command placed at Defcon 2, the only time in US history
Harold Malmgren met MIT President Carl Compton at age 14, who offered him a full scholarship to MIT
Magenta UFO crash in Italy, allegedly confirmed to Malmgren by Richard Bissell
Bluegill Triple Prime test videos declassified with white triangle sanitization applied to footage
FBI destroyed Carl Compton's records on August 30th
Einstein and colleagues predicted quantum entanglement

key_claims.claim: Harold Malmgren directly handled UFO material that fell out of the plume of a Marshall Islands nuclear test
Richard Bissell, deputy director of plans for CIA and chief architect of Area 51, confirmed to Malmgren the existence of the 1933 Magenta UFO crash in Italy
Malmgren was being tracked by Majestic 12 from a young age
UFOs played a part in JFK's death, possibly the number one issue
JFK fully knew all about UFOs long before he became president
Curtis LeMay raised the alert level to Defcon 2 without presidential approval during the Cuban Missile Crisis
Military personnel in the 1950s-60s casually referred to UFOs as 'tagalongs' because they were so accustomed to seeing them
UFO secrecy was bound up in atomic secrecy through the Atomic Energy Commission
Robert Sarbacher stated UFO secrecy is classified at two levels higher than the hydrogen bomb
George W. Bush contemplated UFO disclosure with national security adviser Steven Hadley
The Three-Body Problem novel became mandatory reading among high-level national security advisers regarding UFO disclosure
Navy recovered debris from UFO that was knocked down during Bluegill Triple Prime nuclear test
Despite having blanket Q clearance and presidential clearance, Malmgren was denied access to information about the recovered UFO material
Carl Compton was briefed on UFO technology at Wright Airfield in 1950 according to Robert Sarbacher
Vladimir Nabokov was a CIA asset used to help use literature as propaganda
